mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, systematically reducing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with FEDFUNDS levels.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate profit margins, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates broad-based economic strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, or 20-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of periods where both GDPC1 and RSAFS show positive quarter-over-quarter growth.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid industrial production growth, capacity utilization constraints emerge, leading to input cost inflation and margin compression that suppresses S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The negative relationship reverses to positive during late-cycle expansions, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all tested regimes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity and higher wage expenditures, which support corporate earnings growth and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with PAYEMS growth, or the signal produces negative returns in > 50% of observation windows.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation measured by CPI crosses above 300, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening, which increases borrowing costs and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL crosses above 300, or the threshold signal shows no statistical edge over a linear baseline model.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production growth and housing starts signals sectoral imbalances; when production rises while housing construction falls, it indicates weak downstream demand that drags on equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve predictive accuracy over individual series, or 60-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of periods exhibiting this divergence pattern.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary easing cycles, rising retail sales reflect accommodative liquidity boosting consumer spending, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The positive relationship vanishes or reverses during easing cycles, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 in regime-filtered out-of-sample tests.
